steps.title: Recall what makes two lines parallel
steps.content: Line $k$ is the transversal. From the figure, angles $w$ and $y$ are corresponding angles on lines $r$ and $s$.

steps.title: Use the corresponding angles converse
steps.content: If corresponding angles are congruent, then the two lines cut by the transversal are parallel. Since $w=160$, the statement $y=160$ would make $w$ and $y$ congruent, proving $r \parallel s$.

steps.title: Check the other options
steps.content:
• $x=20$ follows from $w=160$ at the same intersection, so it gives no information about line $s$.
  • $w+y=180$ would make $y=20$, not equal to the corresponding angle $w$.
  • $y+z=180$ describes a linear pair at line $s$, which can be true whether or not the lines are parallel.
The sufficient condition is $y=160$, so B is correct.
